studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Cost and outcome measures are taken at baseline, at the end of the intervention (after 8 weeks), and at 6 and 12 months follow-ups via online questionnaires.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: undergoes a six-week online training (6 lessons, one per week, ~45 mins each) with supportive emails (guided self-help) and optional SMS. A 7th lesson takes place 4 weeks after the final lesson. Control group: receives materials for psychoeducation in the field of depression, and gets access to the online training after the 12-month measurement point.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: Depressive symptoms measured by the Center for Epidemiological Studies Depression Scale (CES-D) / Allgemeine Depressionsskala (ADS). Secondary Outcomes: Demographic questionnaire, Worry (Penn State-Worry-Questionnaire, PSWQ, short version), Activation (Behavioural Activation Depression Scale, BA-DS, short version), Problem solving (Social Problem Solving Inventory, short version), Long-term blood sugar level (HbA1c-value), Problem areas in diabetes (PAID, short version), Acceptance and Action Diabetes Questionnaire (AADQ), Diabetes Self-Management Questionnaire (DSMQ), Depression (HADS-D), Patient Questionnaire on Therapy Expectation and Evaluation (Pathev), Negative effects of online health trainings (INEP), Attitude toward seeking professional help scale (ATSPHS), Satisfaction with the program (ZUF-8), Life quality (EuroQol, SF-12), and Questionnaire for costs associated with psychiatric Illness (TiC-P).

audit.study_title: Internet-based programme to treat depressive symptoms of patients with diabetes mellitus type 1 and type 2
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
